title: Precision Oncology — Matching Therapy to Tumor

segments.label: 01 / 12 · HOOK
title: THE MATCH WAS REAL.
THE RESPONSE WAS NOT.
segments.body_html:
KRAS G12C detected→sotorasib prescribed→shrinks 4 months→grows again

Two patients, one alteration, two different failures. This gap — between matched and responded — is the whole subject of this chapter.

beats.text: A 58-year-old never-smoker. Metastatic lung cancer. Genomic profiling returns a KRAS G12C mutation — an actionable alteration, with approved drugs. She starts sotorasib. The tumor shrinks for four months. Then it grows back. Down the hall, a second patient with the exact same mutation gets the same drug. Zero shrinkage from day one. Same biomarker, same drug, two different failures. The match was real in both cases. The response was not. That gap is everything this chapter is about.

segments.label: 02 / 12 · THE STAKES
title: FROM TISSUE TYPE
TO MOLECULAR FEATURE.
segments.body_html:
OLD
same drug for same organ
→
NOW
same drug for same mutation

Precision oncology reframes "biological variability" as molecular. The cancer's genetic features, more than its tissue, predict which drugs work.

beats.text: For most of oncology's history, drugs were assigned by tissue type. This chemotherapy for everyone with this cancer, regardless of molecular features. Some responded. Others didn't. The difference was called biological variability — a phrase that meant the field didn't know why. Precision oncology is the reframing of that variability as molecular. The insight: the cancer's genetic and protein features, more than its tissue of origin, predict which drugs will work. Sequence the tumor. Find the molecular feature. Prescribe the matched drug.

segments.label: 03 / 12 · ACTIONABLE ALTERATIONS
title: PRESENT IS NOT
THE SAME AS ACTIONABLE.
segments.body_html:
POINT MUTATIONSEGFR exon-19, BRAF V600E, KRAS G12C
GENE FUSIONSALK, ROS1, RET, NTRK
SIGNATURESMSI-H, TMB — functional readouts, not single genes
Actionable = present and linked by clinical evidence to a drug that helps.

beats.text: An actionable alteration is the unit of precision oncology: a molecular feature for which a matched therapy exists and has demonstrated benefit. Actionable features include point mutations like EGFR exon-19 deletion and BRAF V600E, gene fusions in ALK, ROS1, RET, and NTRK, copy-number changes like HER2 amplification, and functional signatures like microsatellite instability and tumor mutational burden. The word actionable is load-bearing. It does not mean present. A mutation you can detect but cannot exploit is interesting biology, not an actionable alteration.

segments.label: 04 / 12 · PREDICTIVE VS PROGNOSTIC
title: THE DISTINCTION
THAT DRIVES EVERYTHING.
segments.body_html:
PROGNOSTIC BIOMARKERTells you how the patient will fare — regardless of which treatment is given. Forecasts natural history.
PREDICTIVE BIOMARKERTells you whether a specific therapy will work in this patient. EGFR exon-19 predicts osimertinib response. BRCA1/2 predicts PARP inhibitor benefit.

Conflating the two produces a specific clinical error: treating a marker of aggressive biology as if it were a drug target.

beats.text: The single most important distinction in this chapter — the one students most consistently collapse — is between a predictive biomarker and a prognostic biomarker. A prognostic biomarker tells you how the patient will fare, regardless of treatment. It forecasts natural history. A predictive biomarker tells you whether a specific therapy will work. EGFR exon-19 deletion predicts response to osimertinib. BRCA1 and BRCA2 germline mutations predict response to PARP inhibitors through synthetic lethality. Precision oncology runs on predictive biomarkers. Conflating the two produces a specific clinical error: treating a marker of aggressive biology as if it were a drug target. The feature predicts bad outcomes not because it drives a blockable pathway, but because it marks an inherently aggressive tumor type. Treating it does nothing.

segments.label: 05 / 12 · TUMOR-AGNOSTIC THERAPY
title: THE BIOLOGY IS WHAT MATTERS.
THE TISSUE IS INCIDENTAL.
segments.body_html:
MSI-H→pembrolizumab · 2017→30–50% response
NTRK fusion→larotrectinib · 2018→>75% response
BRAF V600E→dabrafenib + trametinib · 2022→tissue still matters

beats.text: The most evolved expression of predictive-biomarker logic is tumor-agnostic therapy: a drug approved for any cancer carrying a specific molecular feature, regardless of where in the body it originated. The first tumor-agnostic approval was pembrolizumab for microsatellite-instability-high tumors in 2017 — approved based on response rates of roughly 30 to 50 percent spanning colorectal, endometrial, gastric, and other sites. Then larotrectinib for NTRK fusion-positive tumors, with response rates above 75 percent across all histologies. The claim embedded in every tumor-agnostic approval: the biology is what matters; the tissue is incidental. That claim is powerful. It is also not without limits.

segments.label: 06 / 12 · TISSUE STILL MATTERS
title: SAME MUTATION,
PROFOUNDLY DIFFERENT BIOLOGY.
segments.body_html:
MELANOMA · BRAF V600EResponse rate >60%. EGFR feedback is absent. BRAF + MEK inhibition is durable.
COLORECTAL · BRAF V600EWeak single-agent response. Abundant EGFR signaling provides a bypass route. Must add anti-EGFR antibody.

"Agnostic" in the regulatory label does not mean uniform in the biology.

beats.text: The BRAF V600E case is the clearest illustration of why tissue matters even within a tumor-agnostic framework. In melanoma, BRAF V600E predicts a strong, durable response to BRAF plus MEK inhibition — response rates above 60 percent. In colorectal cancer, the same mutation responds poorly to single-agent BRAF inhibition. The reason: colorectal cancers have abundant EGFR signaling that melanomas do not, and that signaling provides a bypass route around BRAF blockade. Feedback reactivation of EGFR restores the signal the drug blocked. The same mutation, the same approved drug class, profoundly different biology. The tissue that was supposed to be incidental turns out to matter after all.

segments.label: 07 / 12 · TRIAL DESIGNS
title: THE DESIGN DEFINES
THE EVIDENCE CLAIM.
segments.body_html:
BASKETMany tumor types, one molecular alteration → tumor-agnostic claim. Larotrectinib reached approval this way.
UMBRELLAOne cancer type, many molecular arms → tissue-specific matching. Lung-MAP is the classic example.
PLATFORMPermanent infrastructure, arms added and dropped over time → continuous evidence. I-SPY 2 in breast cancer.

beats.text: Precision oncology needed new trial designs because the molecular subgroups it works with are small and scattered across many cancer types. Basket trials enroll patients by molecular alteration across many tumor types — the design that produces tumor-agnostic claims. Umbrella trials work in the opposite direction: they enroll one cancer type and subdivide patients into arms by molecular feature. Lung-MAP is the classic example. Platform trials maintain permanent infrastructure and add or drop treatment arms over time as evidence accumulates. Reading a precision-oncology result requires knowing which design produced it. A basket result supports a tumor-agnostic claim. Citing a basket result to justify treatment in a tumor type not represented in the basket is an extrapolation, not an evidence application.

segments.label: 08 / 12 · TARGET VS DEPENDENCY
title: TARGET PRESENCE
IS NOT TARGET DEPENDENCE.
segments.body_html:
MATCHEDthe alteration is present and the drug binds
≠
RESPONDEDthe tumor depended on that target and kept depending on it

Treatment is selection pressure. It selects for cells capable of routing around the blockade — not a static tumor, but a population under selection.

beats.text: The productive reframing separates the target from the tumor's dependence on that target over time. At baseline, the cancer was driven by KRAS G12C signaling; sotorasib blocked it, and the tumor shrank. The hypothesis — this tumor depends on KRAS G12C — was confirmed by the response. Resistance emerged not because the drug stopped binding, but because the tumor evolved. Treatment is selection pressure. It acts on a population under selection, selecting for cells capable of routing around the blockade. New downstream mutations restored the MAPK signal; a subclone adopted a different driver entirely. The drug still engaged its target. The cancer found another way to grow.

segments.label: 09 / 12 · PRIMARY RESISTANCE
title: CO-OCCURRING ALTERATIONS
BREAK THE DEPENDENCY.
segments.body_html:
KRAS G12C present+parallel oncogenic driver active→blocking KRAS changes nothing
KRAS G12C inhibitors: 35–40% response in lung. EGFR and ALK inhibitors: 60–80%. The difference is oncogene addiction.

beats.text: The second patient — same mutation, never responded — is a different story. The most likely explanation is a co-occurring alteration that made the cancer independent of KRAS G12C signaling from the start. If a tumor carries a loss-of-function mutation in a tumor-suppressor pathway, or has activated a parallel oncogenic pathway, blocking KRAS G12C changes nothing. The proliferative signal arrives through a channel the drug doesn't touch. The biomarker was present; the dependency was not. KRAS G12C inhibitors produce response rates in the 35 to 40 percent range for lung adenocarcinoma — well below the 60 to 80 percent seen with EGFR or ALK inhibitors. KRAS-mutant cancers are biologically more heterogeneous, with more co-occurring alterations and more available escape routes.

segments.label: 10 / 12 · ONCOGENE ADDICTION
title: SINGLE-DRIVER CANCERS
RESPOND. MULTI-DRIVER ONES RESIST.
segments.body_html:
HIGH ONCOGENE ADDICTIONCML: BCR-ABL → imatinib >90% response. EGFR lung: >60%. NTRK fusions: >75%. HER2 breast: transformed prognosis.
LOW ONCOGENE ADDICTIONPancreatic: KRAS + TP53 + SMAD4 — none cleanly druggable. GBM: many alterations, no dominant dependency. TNBC: heterogeneous drivers.

Precision oncology works when the features measured correspond to a genuine, durable dependency the matched drug reliably disrupts.

beats.text: The pattern of success and failure across precision oncology is not random. It follows from the biology. Single-driver, oncogene-addicted cancers have responded most dramatically. Chronic myeloid leukemia with BCR-ABL: imatinib produced response rates above 90 percent and changed a disease with median survival measured in years to one managed as a chronic condition. NTRK fusion-positive tumors: larotrectinib response rates above 75 percent, with responses so durable that some are described as potentially curative. In these settings, the cancer has concentrated its survival dependency on one molecular driver, with limited escape options. Multi-driver, microenvironment-dominated cancers have resisted. Pancreatic adenocarcinoma has been comprehensively sequenced; its dominant drivers — KRAS, TP53, SMAD4, CDKN2A — are none of them cleanly druggable.

segments.label: 11 / 12 · THESIS
title: THE MATCH IS THE HYPOTHESIS.
THE RESPONSE IS THE TEST.
segments.body_html:
The report says what alteration is present. It does not say whether the tumor depends on it — or whether that dependency will persist under selective pressure.

Still open: patient-derived organoids, AI-integrated multi-omic models, functional assays of pathway dependence. None has yet reached positive predictive value high enough to act on reliably.

beats.text: Three possible outcomes across the whole population of KRAS G12C patients: durable benefit, transient benefit followed by resistance, and no benefit at all from day one. The match identified a candidate dependency. Whether each patient's tumor actually depended on it — and kept depending on it — was an empirical question that the drug answered. This is why matched and responded are different measurements. The report says what alteration is present. It does not say whether the tumor depends on it. It does not say whether that dependency will persist under selective pressure. The field is working toward pre-treatment tools that would close this gap: organoid testing, AI-integrated multi-omic models, functional assays of pathway dependence. None has yet reached reliability. Until they do, the match remains the hypothesis, and the response remains the test.

segments.label: 12 / 12 · CLOSE
title: SEQUENCE THE TUMOR.
THEN ASK WHAT IT DEPENDS ON.
segments.body_html:
MATCHED ≠ RESPONDED//TARGET PRESENCE ≠ DEPENDENCE//TISSUE STILL MATTERS

Cancer Research · Chapter 5 · Precision Oncology — Matching Therapy to Tumor

beats.text: That's the frame for everything in precision oncology. Sequence the tumor and you learn what alterations are present. That is the beginning, not the end. The match is a hypothesis about what the tumor depends on. The response is how you test it. Matched is not the same as responded. Target presence is not the same as target dependence. And tissue still matters, even when the label says agnostic. The goal is a pre-treatment assay that closes the gap — that makes the match close to a guarantee rather than a prediction. We're not there yet.

Full voiced lecture deck — Cancer Research series.

A 58-year-old never-smoker. Metastatic lung cancer. Genomic profiling returns a KRAS G12C mutation — an actionable alteration, with approved drugs. She starts sotorasib. The tumor shrinks for four months. Then it grows back. Down the hall, a second patient with the exact same mutation gets the same drug. Zero shrinkage from day one. Same biomarker, same drug, two different failures. The match was real in both cases. The response was not. That gap is everything this chapter is about. For most of oncology's history, drugs were assigned by tissue type. This chemotherapy for everyone with this cancer, regardless of molecular features. Precision oncology is the reframing of that variability as molecular. An actionable alteration is the unit of precision oncology: a molecular feature for which a matched therapy exists and has demonstrated benefit. The single most important distinction: a predictive biomarker tells you whether a specific therapy will work; a prognostic biomarker tells you how the patient will fare regardless of treatment. Conflating the two produces a specific clinical error. The most evolved expression of predictive-biomarker logic is tumor-agnostic therapy. The first approval was pembrolizumab for MSI-H tumors in 2017. The BRAF V600E case shows why tissue matters even within a tumor-agnostic framework. Precision oncology needed new trial designs: basket trials for tumor-agnostic claims, umbrella trials for tissue-specific matching, platform trials for continuous evidence generation.
